purpose.summary: The COPAUS0C program schedules a PSB (Program Specification Block) for IMS database interaction. It handles scheduling, potential rescheduling, and error conditions, setting a flag to indicate successful scheduling or displaying an error message if scheduling fails.
purpose.business_context: This program is likely part of an IMS-based system, possibly related to transaction processing or data access within a larger business application.

called_programs.purpose: Displays an error message on the screen if PSB scheduling fails.

paragraphs.purpose: The SCHEDULE-PSB paragraph is responsible for scheduling a Program Specification Block (PSB) for IMS database interaction. It begins by attempting to schedule the PSB using the EXEC DLI SCHD command with the PSB-NAME. The NODHABEND option specifies that the program should not abnormally terminate if the PSB is unavailable. The DIBSTAT field is then moved to IMS-RETURN-CODE to capture the status of the scheduling operation. If the PSB has been scheduled more than once (PSB-SCHEDULED-MORE-THAN-ONCE), the paragraph terminates the current PSB and schedules it again. If the scheduling is successful (STATUS-OK), the IMS-PSB-SCHD flag is set to TRUE. Otherwise, WS-ERR-FLG is set to 'Y', an error message is constructed including the IMS-RETURN-CODE, the ACCTIDL field of COPAU0AI is set to -1, and the SEND-PAULST-SCREEN paragraph is performed to display the error message.

error_handling.action: Sets WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', constructs an error message, sets ACCTIDL to -1, and calls SEND-PAULST-SCREEN to display the error.
